summary: Key extracted metrics: Incident response SLA = P1 (Critical) Data center outage, data breach risk <=15 minutes <=4 hours; Incident response SLA = P2 (High) System performance degradation affecting >30% users <=30 minutes <=8 hours; Incident response SLA = P3 (Medium) Minor system issues, no user impact <=2 hours <=24 hours; Incident response SLA = P4 (Low) Documentation updates, routine maintenance <=24 hours <=7 business days; Audit log retention = minimum 180 days; Liquidated damages = 20% of total contract value; Confidentiality survival period = 3 years; Data retention limit = no copies after service completion; Local-only data processing = on-premises only; no cloud/off-site transfer. Detected 5 missing clauses: Missing data breach notification deadline; Missing Party A audit right; Missing force majeure liability allocation; Missing intellectual property ownership; Missing regular security reporting requirement.

review_matrix.recommendation: Review whether the damages cap covers the sensitivity and business value of the protected data.

missing_clauses.title: Missing data breach notification deadline
missing_clauses.evidence: No explicit clause detected. Closest reviewed passage: "P1 (Critical) Data center outage, data breach risk <=15 minutes <=4 hours"
missing_clauses.evidenceAnchor: P1 (Critical) Data center outage, data breach risk <=15 minutes <=4 hours
missing_clauses.risk: Missing data breach notification deadline: The contract does not define how quickly Party B must notify Party A after a data breach, which can delay incident response and regulatory handling. Evidence: "No explicit clause detected. Closest reviewed passage: "P1 (Critical) Data center outage, data breach risk <=15 minutes <=4 hours""
missing_clauses.action: Missing data breach notification deadline: Add a breach notification clause with a concrete deadline, escalation channel, required evidence, and incident owner.
missing_clauses.amendmentDraft: Data Breach Notification. Party B shall notify Party A in writing within 24 hours after becoming aware of any actual or suspected data breach, security incident, unauthorized access, or loss of Confidential Information. The notice must include the time discovered, affected systems, data categories, mitigation steps, responsible incident owner, and follow-up remediation plan. Party B shall provide material updates at least every 24 hours until containment is complete.

missing_clauses.title: Missing Party A audit right
missing_clauses.evidence: No explicit clause detected. Closest reviewed passage: "Access Control: Party B must implement least privilege access. Staff may only access systems necessary for assigned work."
missing_clauses.evidenceAnchor: Access Control: Party B must implement least privilege access. Staff may only access systems necessary for assigned work.
missing_clauses.risk: Missing Party A audit right: Party A lacks an explicit right to inspect Party B data processing practices, making compliance verification difficult. Evidence: "No explicit clause detected. Closest reviewed passage: "Access Control: Party B must implement least privilege access. Staff may only access systems necessary for assigned work.""
missing_clauses.action: Missing Party A audit right: Add an audit-right clause covering audit frequency, scope, notice period, evidence access, and remediation obligations.
missing_clauses.amendmentDraft: Audit Rights. Party A may, upon reasonable prior notice and no more than twice per calendar year unless a security incident occurs, audit Party B's compliance with this agreement. Party B shall provide access to relevant policies, access logs, personnel records, subcontractor records, and technical evidence reasonably necessary to verify data processing, confidentiality, and security controls. Party B shall remediate confirmed findings within a mutually agreed remediation period.

missing_clauses.title: Missing force majeure liability allocation
missing_clauses.evidence: No explicit clause detected. Closest reviewed passage: "9. Dispute Resolution"
missing_clauses.risk: Missing force majeure liability allocation: The contract does not explain which obligations survive force majeure or how liability is allocated during exceptional events. Evidence: "No explicit clause detected. Closest reviewed passage: "9. Dispute Resolution""
missing_clauses.action: Missing force majeure liability allocation: Add a force majeure clause that separates excused delay from confidentiality, security, notification, and mitigation duties.
missing_clauses.amendmentDraft: Force Majeure. Neither party shall be liable for delay caused by events beyond reasonable control, provided that the affected party promptly notifies the other party and uses commercially reasonable efforts to mitigate the impact. Force majeure shall not excuse Party B from confidentiality, data protection, breach notification, access control, return or destruction of data, or incident mitigation obligations.

missing_clauses.title: Missing intellectual property ownership
missing_clauses.evidence: No explicit clause detected. Closest reviewed passage: "P4 (Low) Documentation updates, routine maintenance <=24 hours <=7 business days"
missing_clauses.evidenceAnchor: P4 (Low) Documentation updates, routine maintenance <=24 hours <=7 business days
missing_clauses.risk: Missing intellectual property ownership: Ownership of service deliverables is undefined, which may create disputes over scripts, reports, configurations, or documentation. Evidence: "No explicit clause detected. Closest reviewed passage: "P4 (Low) Documentation updates, routine maintenance <=24 hours <=7 business days""
missing_clauses.action: Missing intellectual property ownership: Add an IP ownership clause defining deliverables, background IP, license scope, source materials, and post-termination rights.
missing_clauses.amendmentDraft: Intellectual Property and Deliverables. Party A shall own all reports, configurations, scripts, documentation, remediation records, and other deliverables specifically created for Party A under this agreement. Each party retains ownership of its pre-existing intellectual property. Party B grants Party A a perpetual, royalty-free license to use any Party B background materials embedded in the deliverables solely as necessary to operate and maintain Party A systems.

missing_clauses.title: Missing regular security reporting requirement
missing_clauses.evidence: No explicit clause detected. Closest reviewed passage: "Access Control: Party B must implement least privilege access. Staff may only access systems necessary for assigned work."
missing_clauses.evidenceAnchor: Access Control: Party B must implement least privilege access. Staff may only access systems necessary for assigned work.
missing_clauses.risk: Missing regular security reporting requirement: Party A does not receive a recurring security reporting commitment, reducing ongoing visibility into vendor controls. Evidence: "No explicit clause detected. Closest reviewed passage: "Access Control: Party B must implement least privilege access. Staff may only access systems necessary for assigned work.""
missing_clauses.action: Missing regular security reporting requirement: Add a reporting clause requiring periodic security summaries, incident statistics, access-review results, and open remediation items.
missing_clauses.amendmentDraft: Security Reporting. Party B shall provide Party A with a written security report at least monthly. The report must include access review results, privileged account changes, incident and near-miss statistics, subcontractor activity, unresolved remediation items, and confirmation that data localization, encryption, access control, and retention requirements remain satisfied.

Missing Party A audit right: Party A lacks an explicit right to inspect Party B data processing practices, making compliance verification difficult. Evidence: "No explicit clause detected. Closest reviewed passage: "Access Control: Party B must implement least privilege access. Staff may only access systems necessary for assigned work.""

Missing force majeure liability allocation: The contract does not explain which obligations survive force majeure or how liability is allocated during exceptional events. Evidence: "No explicit clause detected. Closest reviewed passage: "9. Dispute Resolution""

Missing intellectual property ownership: Ownership of service deliverables is undefined, which may create disputes over scripts, reports, configurations, or documentation. Evidence: "No explicit clause detected. Closest reviewed passage: "P4 (Low) Documentation updates, routine maintenance <=24 hours <=7 business days""

Missing regular security reporting requirement: Party A does not receive a recurring security reporting commitment, reducing ongoing visibility into vendor controls. Liquidated damages are fixed at 20% of total contract value; check whether this cap is sufficient for customer PII, financial records, and internal business data. Party B remains responsible for direct and indirect losses caused by its breach."
action_items: Missing data breach notification deadline: Add a breach notification clause with a concrete deadline, escalation channel, required evidence, and incident owner.
Missing Party A audit right: Add an audit-right clause covering audit frequency, scope, notice period, evidence access, and remediation obligations.
Missing force majeure liability allocation: Add a force majeure clause that separates excused delay from confidentiality, security, notification, and mitigation duties.
Missing intellectual property ownership: Add an IP ownership clause defining deliverables, background IP, license scope, source materials, and post-termination rights.
Missing regular security reporting requirement: Add a reporting clause requiring periodic security summaries, incident statistics, access-review results, and open remediation items.
Preserve the audit-log retention requirement and define inspector, export format, and tamper-evidence controls.
Review whether the damages cap covers the sensitivity and business value of the protected data.
Confirm whether three years is sufficient for the protected data classes.
Preserve the no-copy requirement and define deletion evidence.
Preserve the local-only processing control and define exceptions approval.
Verify that each P1-P4 SLA has an owner, clock-start definition, escalation path, and evidence trail.
